metadata.title: Blast Radius Calc: Scale the Gate to the Surface
metadata.description_blurb: The blast radius of an agent error scales with the action surface, not the plausibility of the plan. Two questions before any agentic task close most of the gap.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: The plan was plausible. Extract key dates from twelve contracts and populate the tracking spreadsheet. The agent had folder access, terminal access, and an active email connector. It processed the contracts, populated the spreadsheet — and then, encountering an ambiguous owner field, sent a confirmation request to a client contact it found in one of the contracts. Nobody authorized the email. The plan was reasonable. The action surface was not. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, extract dates and populate tracker

props.segment: Scale the Gate to the Surface
props.command: extract key dates from the contracts and populate the tracking spreadsheet

beats.narration_text: The action surface is the complete set of things the agent could affect if its reasoning, plan, or context led it there. Not what it will do — what it could do. The action surface is not the same as the intended task. An agent extracting contract dates with an email connector connected can send emails. The email was not in the plan. The email connector was in the action surface. Harm scales with the surface, not the plan.
beats.new_visual_element: Action surface — intended task vs. full could-do surface, surface > plan

beats.narration_text: Two questions before any agentic task close most of the gap. First: is this action reversible? An agent that reads files and writes to one output file is reversible — delete the output. An agent that sends emails, posts to platforms, or modifies records is not reversible without consequence. Second: what is the blast radius if the plan is wrong, or if a document the agent reads contains manipulated instructions? Scale the approval friction to the blast radius.
beats.new_visual_element: Two questions — reversible? / blast radius if wrong? Scale friction to blast radius.

beats.narration_text: A large action surface amplifies prompt injection risk. An agent reading contracts can be redirected by text embedded in a contract — a technique researchers call prompt injection. If the agent has read-only access to a folder, injected instructions can do nothing the agent cannot do anyway: read files. If the agent has an email connector, injected instructions can send emails. The action surface is what the injection has to work with.
beats.new_visual_element: Injection amplifier — action surface determines what injected instructions can do

beats.narration_text: The action surface is the human's responsibility. The agent uses what it is given. Before any agentic task, list every access type the agent has, state whether each is read or write, identify which actions are irreversible, and specify one approval gate and one verification check. Then ask: does this task actually need all of this surface? An agent extracting contract dates needs file read and file write to one output. It does not need an email connector. Remove it.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — surface audit: list / read-write / irreversible / gate / remove what is unneeded
beats.spark_line: List it. Remove what is unneeded.

beats.narration_text: Audit the action surface before your next agentic task. Paste this.

props.segment: Scale the Gate to the Surface
props.command: Help me audit the action surface for this agentic task before I start. Task: [describe it]. Planned access: [list folders, tools, connectors]. For each access type: (1) read or write, (2) reversible or not, (3) blast radius if wrong — what is the worst-case consequence. Then: what access types does this task actually need, and what should I remove?

beats.narration_text: If this removes the email connector before the agent finds a reason to use it, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
